problem_statement: The GovernedPlanningProfile sequence has collected replay/recovery witness evidence and now needs a schema-backed terminal closure certificate that closes the local no-effect promotion evidence ladder without granting runtime promotion or activation authority.
success_criteria: a deterministic validator emits the terminal closure certificate from the current replay/recovery witness
the checked-in local terminal closure certificate matches the deterministic produced certificate
the schema records terminal closure certificate evidence as collected while denying runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success claims, and terminal closure authority

the certificate records passed controls for source replay/recovery validity, all promotion gates satisfied, zero mismatch preservation, rollback and recovery handoff binding, authority denials, Foundation Mode no-effect boundary, and certificate hash binding
the certificate records zero remaining promotion evidence gates while requiring a separate runtime promotion authorization request before activation

focused tests cover schema compatibility, fixture parity, source witness binding, all-gates-satisfied evidence, remaining gate rejection, unsatisfied source rejection, authority-drift rejection, and certificate-boundary drift rejection
non_goals: do not execute, dispatch, replan, replay, or rollback
do not add a router, worker, scheduler, connector, or runtime registration path
do not claim live runtime activation
do not replace the prior replay/recovery witness
constraints: the terminal closure certificate is local, read-only, deterministic, and reversible
runtime promotion activation remains denied until a separate authority-changing action is governed
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved because the validator does not normalize or decompose text
evidence_required: schema-backed local terminal closure certificate fixture
deterministic validator and produced certificate parity
SDLC design and security review artifacts
